name: Easy English Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine shortening, salt, and sugar in a large bowl.
2. Pour boiling water over the ingredients and stir until the shortening melts.
3. Allow the mixture to cool to lukewarm.
4. Sprinkle yeast over the mixture; stir to dissolve.
5. Let stand until the mixture begins to bubble.
6. Gradually add flour and beat until combined.
7. Roll out on a floured board to about 3/8-inch thickness.
8. Cut into rounds using a round cutter (e.g., clean, empty tuna can).
9. Sprinkle cookie sheets with cornmeal.
10. Place muffins fairly far apart on the sheets.
11. Let rise until doubled in bulk.
12. Preheat griddle or electric frypan to 350°F.
13. Lightly sprinkle cornmeal on the griddle.
14. Place muffins on the griddle, rounded side down, and bake for 7 minutes.
15. Turn muffins and bake for another 7 minutes on the other side.
16. Split with a fork and toast or spread with butter under the broiler until bubbles form.
These muffins freeze well. They should be split with a fork before freezing. The recipe can be doubled when preparing muffins for the freezer. When making these, I typically make 5-6 recipes! They are even better than Wolfermans! I could eat them until I pop! Yum!
